channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Gleisi compartilha vídeo que liga Flávio a “submundo do crime”
item.description: Institutions Minister Gleisi Hoffmann shared a video on social media accusing Senator Flávio Bolsonaro of ties to militias and organized crime in Rio de Janeiro, citing Federal Police operations, alleged appointments, and decorations given to militia-linked figures. Bolsonaro denied the claims, stating he had only met one of the individuals mentioned twice. The post comes as polls show Flávio and Lula statistically tied in a potential 2026 presidential runoff.

item.title: Exportações do agro têm em fevereiro melhor resultado para o mês da série
item.description: Brazilian agribusiness exports reached US$12.05 billion in February 2026, the highest monthly figure in the historical series, according to the Ministry of Agriculture. This performance highlights the sector's strengthening in international trade, driven by global demand and the competitiveness of Brazilian products.

item.title: Exportações do agro têm em fevereiro melhor resultado para o mês da série
item.description: Brazilian agribusiness exports reached $12.05 billion in February 2026, the highest monthly figure in the historical series, up 7.4% year-on-year driven by a 9% increase in export volume. Despite a 1.5% decline in average international prices, the agro trade surplus reached $10.5 billion, with China as the top destination (30.5%) and strong growth in soybean, animal protein, and Asian markets like Vietnam and India.

item.title: Mendonça reforça decisão de prisão de Vorcaro; Fux acompanha
item.description: Supreme Court Justice André Mendonça upheld his order to arrest Daniel Vorcaro, founder of Banco Master, with support from Justice Luiz Fux, resulting in a 2-0 vote in the 2nd Chamber. Vorcaro remains in maximum-security isolation at the Federal Penitentiary in Brasília, pending votes from Gilmar Mendes and Nunes Marques. Justice Dias Toffoli recused himself due to prior impartiality concerns in related Master Bank cases.

item.title: Exportações do agro têm em fevereiro melhor resultado para o mês da série histórica
item.description: Brazilian agribusiness exports reached US$12.05 billion in February 2026, the highest historical result for the month, up 7.4% from February 2025, driven by a 9% increase in exported volume. The agricultural trade surplus reached US$10.5 billion, with China as the top destination (30.5%), followed by the European Union and the United States. The soybean complex led exports, followed by animal protein, forest products, coffee, and the sugarcane ethanol complex.

item.title: André Mendonça e Luiz Fux votam para manter prisão preventiva de Vorcaro
item.description: Supreme Court ministers André Mendonça and Luiz Fux voted to maintain the preventive detention of former banker Daniel Vorcaro, accused of financial fraud in the 'Compliance Zero' operation. The trial is being held by the Second Chamber of the STF, with only four votes due to Justice Dias Toffoli's recusal, which could result in a tie and, under internal rules, Vorcaro's release.

item.title: STF tem dois votos para manter prisão de Daniel Vorcaro
item.description: Justices André Mendonça and Luiz Fux voted to maintain the imprisonment of Daniel Vorcaro, owner of Banco Master, and two others before the STF. The trial is underway in the Second Chamber, with votes from Gilmar Mendes and Nunes Marques still pending. In case of a tie, the position most favorable to the accused prevails, as Justice Dias Toffoli has recused himself.

item.title: Sentimento do consumidor nos EUA cai a 55,5 em março, mostra pesquisa preliminar
item.description: The University of Michigan's U.S. consumer sentiment index fell from 56.6 in February to 55.5 in March, according to preliminary data released Friday. The decline was slightly more than analysts had expected (55.3). Twelve-month inflation expectations remained steady at 3.4%, while the five-year outlook edged down from 3.3% to 3.2%.

item.title: PSD aciona STF contra lei que regulamenta eleição indireta para governador do Rio; entenda
item.description: The PSD filed a Direct Action of Unconstitutionality (ADI) with the STF against Rio de Janeiro’s law regulating the indirect election for governor, specifically challenging open voting and the 24-hour incompatibility deadline for candidates. The party argues these provisions violate the Federal Constitution and enable political power abuse, benefiting allies of Governor Cláudio Castro, such as Nicola Miccione and Douglas Ruas, as well as PT’s André Ceciliano. The law was signed hours before by Castro, who will resign in April to run for Senate, creating a power vacuum to be filled by a slate chosen by the 70 Alerj deputies through the end of 2026.

item.title: PF deve pedir mais prazo a Mendonça para concluir inquérito do Banco Master
item.description: The Federal Police is expected to request additional time from Supreme Court Justice André Mendonça to conclude the Banco Master investigation due to the large volume of unanalyzed data, including at least seven seized devices. The PF anticipates new phases of the operation and potentially new suspects, which is already causing concern within the STF over the possible implication of ministers.

item.title: Trama golpista: Primeira Turma do STF julga recursos contra a condenação de grupo que espalhava fake news
item.description: The First Chamber of the STF is reviewing appeals from individuals convicted for their role in the 2022 coup attempt, specifically for spreading false information about electronic voting machines and attacking institutions. Relator Justice Alexandre de Moraes voted to uphold the convictions of Nucleus 4, with sentences ranging from 7 to 17 years in prison. The virtual hearing continues until March 20, unless a request for view or certification interrupts it.

item.title: SUS passa a adotar antibiótico para prevenir sífilis e clamídia
item.description: The SUS has adopted doxycycline 100 mg as post-exposure prophylaxis to prevent syphilis and chlamydia, following approval by Conitec and publication in the Official Gazette. Technical areas have up to 180 days to implement the measure in health units, expanding access to prevention of bacterial STIs among sexually active populations.

item.title: Copa do Brasil define confrontos da 4ª fase; veja duelos e premiação
item.description: The third round of the 2026 Copa do Brasil concluded on Thursday (12), determining the 24 clubs advancing to the fourth round, which will decide the 12 spots in the next stage. The matchups for the fourth round have already been set and will take place soon, maintaining the single-elimination format. The tournament continues to offer substantial prize money to participating clubs, encouraging competitive performance.

item.title: Julgamento de Vorcaro: Mendonça e Fux defendem manutenção de preventiva
item.description: Supreme Court ministers André Mendonça and Luiz Fux voted to maintain the preventive detention of banker Daniel Vorcaro in a virtual plenary session. Mendonça, the case's rapporteur, presented his vote on Friday morning, the 13th, and was followed by Fux. Ministers Kassio Nunes Marques and Gilmar Mendes have yet to vote.

item.title: Petrobras adere ao subsídio do governo para diesel
item.description: Petrobras has joined the federal government's diesel subsidy program authorized by MP 1.340/2026, which provides R$0.32 per liter in subsidies and a fiscal impact of R$10 billion for the Treasury. The adhesion is conditional on the ANP publishing operational rules and aims to mitigate international price volatility without passing costs to domestic consumers. The government states the full package, including tax cuts, will impact public accounts by R$30 billion by December 2026, in response to rising oil prices due to Middle East tensions involving the U.S., Israel, and Iran.

item.title: Gilmar Mendes leva para sessão presencial decisão sobre quebra de sigilo de ‘Lulinha’
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Gilmar Mendes requested a review (destaque) of the virtual deliberation on the annulment of data seizure orders targeting Fábio Luís Lula da Silva (Lulinha), transferring the case to an in-person session. Justice Flávio Dino had voted to uphold the annulment of the bulk-approved data seizures by the INSS Congressional Inquiry Committee, citing lack of individualized voting on the 87 requests. Although Dino’s decision prevents the use of already-obtained data in the committee’s final report, the seized information remains in the CPMI’s possession and will now be debated orally by all 11 justices.

item.title: Mendonça inicia julgamento no STF e vota para manter Vorcaro preso
item.description: Ministers André Mendonça and Luiz Fux of the STF voted to maintain the preventive detention of Daniel Vorcaro, former Banco Master banker, in a virtual trial of the Second Chamber. Vorcaro was re-arrested on March 4 after the Federal Police found messages on his phone indicating a plan to fake an assault against journalist Lauro Jardim to intimidate him. He is suspected of leading a private militia called 'A Turma' and had previously been arrested in November for attempting to flee the country.

item.title: Mendonça e Fux votam por manter a prisão de Vorcaro
item.description: Justices André Mendonça and Luiz Fux voted to maintain the imprisonment of Daniel Vorcaro, former president of Banco Master, in the Second Chamber of the STF, with Justice Dias Toffoli recused. The ruling pertains to Operation Compliance Zero, investigating organized crime, money laundering, and other offenses tied to the bank’s scheme. Mendonça stated his vote supported the precautionary measure, except for Luiz Phillipi Machado de Moraes Mourão, who died in custody.

item.title: André Mendonça e Fux votam para manter prisão preventiva de Vorcaro
item.description: Supreme Court ministers André Mendonça and Luiz Fux voted to maintain the preventive detention of former banker Daniel Vorcaro of Banco Master in the 2nd Chamber. As rapporteur, Mendonça and Fux supported keeping Vorcaro, Fabiano Zettel, and Marilson Roseno da Silva in prison over alleged involvement in the Master scheme. The vote remains pending from Kássio Nunes Marques and Gilmar Mendes, while Dias Toffoli recused himself, raising the possibility of a tie that would favor Vorcaro under STF rules favoring the defendant in penal ties.

item.title: Caso Master: Mendonça e Fux votam para manter prisão de Vorcaro; placar está 2x0
item.description: Supreme Court Ministers André Mendonça and Luiz Fux voted to maintain the preventive detention of banker Daniel Vorcaro in the third phase of Operation Compliance Zero, resulting in a 2-0 partial score. Gilmar Mendes and Nunes Marques have yet to vote, with a deadline until May 20. Mendonça argued that evidence from the first seized phone justifies the measure and rejected the defense’s claim that the 'A Turma' WhatsApp group was merely casual, citing the inclusion of a federal police officer and the organization’s latent threat.

item.title: Mendonça abre julgamento e vota para manter Vorcaro em presídio de segurança máxima; Fux acompanha
item.description: Minister André Mendonça initiated the vote at the Supreme Federal Court to keep banker Daniel Vorcaro in preventive detention at the Federal Penitentiary of Brasília, with Minister Luiz Fux supporting the motion, resulting in a 2-0 score. The trial also examines the continued detention of Vorcaro’s brother-in-law and an alleged leader of his private militia, 'A Turma'. The verdict is expected by next Friday, the 20th, pending votes from Gilmar Mendes and Nunes Marques.

item.title: Serviços crescem 3,3% ante janeiro de 2025, 22º avanço consecutivo
item.description: Brazilian services grew 3.3% in January 2026 compared to January 2025, marking the 22nd consecutive monthly increase, driven primarily by information and communication (6.5%) and professional, administrative, and complementary services (5%). Tourism declined 1.1% month-over-month (Dec 2025 to Jan 2026) but rose 3.5% year-over-year, with strong growth in Mato Grosso (20.7%) and Amazonas (13.7%). The diffusion index for services was 48.2%, meaning less than half of the 166 service types surveyed showed positive growth.

item.title: Sem Toffoli, empate no julgamento da prisão favorecerá Vorcaro
item.description: The STF's 2nd Chamber is reviewing the preventive detention of Daniel Vorcaro, founder of Banco Master, after rapporteur André Mendonça authorized the arrest. Justice Dias Toffoli's recusal reduces the panel to four ministers, raising the possibility of a tie, which would favor Vorcaro under STF tie-breaking rules. His defense argues he cooperated with investigations and requests less restrictive measures, such as house arrest with an electronic monitor.

item.title: Justiça Eleitoral pelo Brasil: TRE-AM abrirá três postos de atendimento em áreas da capital
item.description: The TRE-AM will open three temporary assistance posts in Manaus to support voter registration updates until May 6. The first post opens on March 16 at PAC Cidade Leste, followed by locations at Sumaúma Shopping and Vasco Vasques Convention Center on April 6. Services include voter registration, address transfer, data updates, title regularization, and biometric enrollment, requiring prior appointment via the TRE-AM website.

item.title: Bolsonaro é diagnosticado com broncopneumonia e ficará internado em Brasília
item.description: Former Brazilian President Jair Bolsonaro was diagnosed with bronchopneumonia and taken to DF Star Hospital in Brasília after falling ill at the Papuda Penitentiary Complex. According to his son, Senator Flávio Bolsonaro, he experienced chills, vomiting, and low oxygen saturation, prompting hospitalization.

item.title: Após internação, defesa de Bolsonaro critica Moraes por negar domiciliar
item.description: Bolsonaro's defense criticized Justice Alexandre de Moraes after the former president was hospitalized with fever, vomiting, and low oxygenation, arguing that his severe condition had been previously predicted in medical reports that supported a denied request for house arrest. Lawyer Paulo Cunha Bueno contrasted the decision with the humanitarian house arrest granted to Fernando Collor de Mello, asserting Bolsonaro's medical condition is more serious.

item.title: Nova pesquisa mostra como está o duelo entre Lula e Flávio no estado mais decisivo do país
item.description: Nova pesquisa mostra como está o duelo entre Lula e Flávio no estado mais decisivo do país

item.title: Cármen Lúcia nega no mesmo dia 3 habeas corpus para soltar Bolsonaro
item.description: Minister Cármen Lúcia of the Brazilian Supreme Court denied three habeas corpus requests in a single day aimed at releasing Jair Bolsonaro, who is serving a 27-year and three-month sentence at Papudinha for his role in the coup plot. The rulings were based on the inadmissibility of third-party habeas corpus without defense authorization and the impossibility of challenging a minister’s act via HC. Bolsonaro remains incarcerated, with detention conditions deemed fully satisfactory by Minister Alexandre de Moraes.

item.title: Em meio à guerra no Irã, colonos israelenses intensificam ataques a palestinos na Cisjordânia
item.description: Since the onset of the Israeli-U.S. offensive against Iran on February 28, Israeli settlers have intensified violent attacks against Palestinians in the occupied West Bank, resulting in at least five deaths and numerous incidents of assault, property destruction, and resource theft. Human rights groups like B'Tselem and Yesh Din warn that the war is being used as a pretext to deepen ethnic cleansing, with Israeli military forces either enabling or ignoring settler violence. The European Union has condemned the violence and urged Israel to take immediate action to hold perpetrators accountable and protect Palestinian civilians.

item.title: Guerra no Oriente Médio: governo faz projeções e calcula que arrecadaria quase R$ 100 bilhões a mais neste ano no pior cenário
item.description: The Brazilian Ministry of Finance projected that, in the worst-case scenario of the Middle East war — with oil averaging US$100 per barrel — federal net revenue would rise by R$96.6 billion in 2026 due to royalties, taxes on oil company profits, and indirect effects on the tax base. This scenario would also push inflation above 4% and increase the trade surplus by US$10.3 billion. The government warns that more extreme scenarios could trigger stagflation, harming both global and Brazilian growth.

item.title: Policiais usam pano para evitar imagens de Bolsonaro; veja vídeo
item.description: Police officers used a cloth to shield the transfer of former President Jair Bolsonaro from the ambulance to DF Star Hospital in Brasília, aiming to prevent him from being photographed or filmed. Bolsonaro fell ill on the morning of March 13, 2026, showing fever, low blood oxygen, and vomiting, and was admitted at 8:50 a.m. Doctor Cláudio Birolini, traveling from São Paulo to assess him, confirmed he had already ordered clinical exams.

item.title: Importadores de cacau têm prazo menor para benefício fiscal após MP
item.description: Provisional Measure 1.341/2026 reduces from two years to six months the period for companies exporting products made with foreign cocoa to benefit from tax exemptions on the import of whole or broken cocoa, including exemption or suspension of Import Tax, IPI, PIS, and Cofins. The measure aims to benefit Brazilian cocoa producers by increasing their sales to these companies. Congress has up to 120 days to review the MP, which, if approved, will become a definitive law.

item.title: Volume de serviços cresce 0,3% em janeiro e volta a nível recorde, diz IBGE
item.description: Brazil's service sector volume rose 0.3% in January 2026, exceeding the market forecast of 0.1%, and returned to the record level seen in October and November 2025. Growth was driven mainly by 'other services' (+3.7%) and information and communication (+1%), while services to households declined 1.2%. The IBGE also revised December 2024's figure from -0.4% to -0.2%.

item.title: Recife, cenário de ‘O Agente Secreto’, exibe seu orgulho nordestino antes do Oscar
item.description: Recife is experiencing a surge of Northeastern pride due to the international success of 'O Agente Secreto', a film by Kleber Mendonça Filho set in the city during Brazil's military dictatorship. The movie is nominated for four Oscars, including Best Picture and Best Actor for Wagner Moura, and has sparked a boom in film-themed tourism, merchandise sales, and guided tours of filming locations. For Pernambucan historians and filmmakers, the film challenges stereotypical portrayals of the Northeast as backward and subordinate, presenting Recife as cosmopolitan, intellectual, and architecturally significant.

item.title: MPT defende limites à negociação coletiva que amplie jornada em ambiente insalubre
item.description: The Superior Labor Court (TST) held a public hearing to discuss the validity of collective bargaining agreements that extend working hours in unhealthy environments, under the repetitive appeal procedure (Theme 149). The Labor Public Prosecutor’s Office (MPT), represented by Deputy Attorney General Francisco Gerson de Lima, advocates for limits on collective bargaining to ensure such agreements do not disregard the actual verification of unhealthy conditions, stressing that the CLT protects workers’ health despite being called 'outdated'.

item.title: Pesquisa Real Time Big Data testa cenários para o governo de MG, veja os números
item.description: The Real Time Big Data poll shows Senator Cleitinho (Republicanos) leading simulations for the Minas Gerais governorship in 2026, with Rodrigo Pacheco (PSD/União Brasil) as his main rival. When Cleitinho is excluded, Pacheco takes the lead, while Alexandre Kalil (PDT) emerges as an alternative in scenarios without both senators. Rejection data was not detailed in the provided excerpt.

item.title: ‘Arrumando confusão com os EUA’: a reação de Flávio a veto de Moraes a assessor de Trump
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro criticized Minister Alexandre de Moraes for reversing the authorization of a visit by Trump adviser Darren Beattie to imprisoned former president Jair Bolsonaro, claiming Moraes is 'stirring up trouble with the United States'. He called Moraes 'toxic' and accused him of damaging the Judiciary's image, suggesting hidden motives. The reversal followed a warning from Brazil’s Foreign Ministry about potential U.S. interference in the 2026 elections, as Beattie had no confirmed diplomatic agenda — only participation in a critical minerals forum.

item.title: Visita ao Poder360: Carlos Antônio Vieira
item.description: Carlos Antônio Vieira, 65, president of Caixa Econômica Federal, visited the Poder360 headquarters in Brasília on Thursday, March 12, 2026, and delivered a lecture to the second cohort of the outlet’s Journalism Trainee Program.

item.title: Médico de Bolsonaro vai a Brasília para avaliar saúde do ex-presidente
item.description: Former President Jair Bolsonaro was taken to DF Star Hospital in Brasília after exhibiting chills, vomiting, fever, and low blood oxygen. His personal physician, Cláudio Birolini, is traveling from São Paulo to assess his condition and has requested medical exams. Bolsonaro remains detained at the 19th Military Police Battalion in DF (Papudinha) and has a history of health issues stemming from the 2018 stabbing attack, including bilateral hernia surgery in late 2025.

item.title: Carlos Antônio Vieira fala aos trainees em jornalismo do Poder360
item.description: Carlos Antônio Vieira, president of Caixa Econômica Federal, visited Poder360's headquarters in Brasília and delivered a lecture to the second cohort of the Journalism Trainees Program, discussing Caixa's initiatives and the bank's stance on betting and cryptoassets. The program, which started in January and runs through April 2026, lasts three months and is sponsored by XP Inc. and FenaSaúde, including visits to government agencies and integration with the newsroom.

item.title: Novos lançamentos que dominam as plataformas de streaming e já estão entre os mais assistidos
item.description: New film and series releases in 2026 are rapidly capturing Brazilian audiences, quickly rising to the top of viewership rankings on platforms like Netflix, Prime Video, Disney+, Max, and Globoplay. This trend highlights how high-impact premieres can attract millions of viewers in a short time.

item.title: As intenções de voto de Lula e Flávio Bolsonaro em Minas Gerais, segundo nova pesquisa
item.description: Lula leads with 35% of voting intentions in Minas Gerais, followed by Flávio Bolsonaro with 31%, within the two-point margin of error. Governor Romeu Zema ranks third with 15%, while other candidates trail below 5%. The survey interviewed 2,000 voters between March 10 and 11, with TSE registration BR-05044/2026.

item.title: Camisa 2 do Brasil estreia parceria com marca de Michael Jordan; confira fotos
item.description: Nike unveiled Brazil's 2026 World Cup away jersey in partnership with Jordan Brand, Michael Jordan's division. The kit features predator-inspired patterns on a blue base with black stripes, green-yellow accents, and the Jumpman logo. It debuts in the friendly against France on March 26 in the U.S., with fan and player versions priced at R$449,99 and R$749,99 respectively.

item.title: Daniel Vorcaro continuará preso e fará delação premiada?
item.description: The STF's Second Chamber ruling on Daniel Vorcaro's preventive detention and potential plea bargain will be discussed on VEJA's 'Os Três Poderes' program, revealing how his secrets alarm Brasília authorities. The episode also covers recent polls showing Flávio Bolsonaro consolidating against Lula, with Jair Bolsonaro directing his son's pre-campaign from prison, alongside analysis of the Iran war, oil crisis due to Strait of Ormuz closure, and Lula government measures to curb fuel price increases.

item.title: Áudio: Comissão de Educação debaterá 10 anos do Marco Legal da Primeira Infância
item.description: The Senate's Education and Culture Commission will hold a public hearing to discuss the 10th anniversary of the Legal Framework for Early Childhood (Law 13.257/2016), which establishes guidelines for health, education, and social protection policies for children up to six years old. Senator Teresa Leitão (PT-PE) stated the debate could help improve and implement the legislation. The hearing date has not yet been set.

item.title: Governo Trump propõe que Brasil receba estrangeiros capturados nos EUA e exige plano para acabar com PCC e CV
item.description: The Trump administration proposed that Brazil accept foreign nationals detained in the U.S. in its prisons, similar to El Salvador’s model, and demanded a Brazilian plan to combat PCC, CV, Hezbollah, and Chinese criminal groups. The proposal also includes sharing biometric data of refugees and foreigners in Brazil as part of transnational security cooperation, ahead of Lula’s expected visit to the U.S. in April 2026.

item.title: Serviços avançam 0,3% em janeiro e igualam patamar recorde
item.description: Brazil's services volume rose 0.3% in January compared to December, matching the historical record high of the series initiated in 2011, according to IBGE data released on March 13, 2026. The sector remains active at elevated levels, above pre-pandemic benchmarks, with notable contributions from transport and logistics, and continues its year-on-year growth trend. Services constitute the largest share of Brazil's GDP and serve as a key indicator of national economic activity.

item.title: Enviado de Trump pediu reunião no Itamaraty após aval para visitar Bolsonaro
item.description: Darren Beattie, a senior U.S. State Department advisor on Brazil policy, was authorized by Supreme Court Minister Alexandre de Moraes to meet with former President Jair Bolsonaro on March 18, though Bolsonaro’s defense requested rescheduling to March 16 or 17 due to diplomatic commitments. The U.S. Embassy in Brasília sought a meeting at the Itamaraty after the authorization, but the Brazilian Foreign Ministry stated it never received a formal request and warned the visit could constitute interference in Brazil’s internal affairs during an election year.

item.title: Bartomeu niega haber contratado perfiles falsos para criticar a sus rivales del Barça e insiste en que solo quería “monitorizar” las redes sociales
item.description: Former Barcelona president Josep Maria Bartomeu appeared voluntarily in court amid the Barçagate scandal, accused of hiring fake social media profiles to attack political rivals within the club. He denied the allegations, stating the goal was merely to monitor online discourse after Neymar’s departure to PSG and during the peak of the Catalan independence process in 2017. The case involves i3Ventures and the alleged crime of disloyal administration.

item.title: Novas investidas dos EUA contra o Brasil são mais difíceis de serem rebatidas
item.description: The Brazilian government is facing new challenges from the U.S., including a request for a Trump aide to visit imprisoned ex-president Bolsonaro and efforts to label the PCC and Comando Vermelho as terrorist organizations. While Itamaraty has denounced these actions as interference, Lula’s advisors say countering these provocations is now harder, especially given public concern over security and uncertainty about whether Trump fully endorses the moves. The government is choosing to avoid provocations ahead of a planned meeting between Lula and Trump at the White House.
